titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.36

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=630 status=live modified=2022-11-01 13:09:07.122634+00 | translation moa=631 status=live modified=2022-11-01 13:09:06.18635+00

div.p:
Omnibus Christi Fidelibus presentes Litteras visuris vel audituris . Ranulfus decanus Laudonie
Iohannes . persona . de Pentkatland . Robertus . persona . de Methfen et Ricardus de Kelchov rector ecclesie de Kin
gor . eternam in domino Salutem . Cum auctoritate venerabilis patris nostri Willelmi dei gratia Episcopi sancti
Andree taxatio vicarie de Hadintona nobis esset commissa . secundum estimationem virorum fide dignorum
qui iuramento interposito super hoc deposuerunt uisis et inspectis attestationibus in subscriptis portio
nibus duximus taxandam . presente et consentiente dauid de Berwyc tunc vicario . Scilicet in oblatio
nibus . Ouentionibus . tocius parochie de uiuis et mortuis . tam de capellis quam de matrici ecclesia . et omnibus
decimis negotiationis burgensium et aliorum negotiatorum omnium . et conductorum et omnibus decimis
curtilagiorum et pomeriorum . infra burgum cum medietate decime feni . Easdem portiones Sibi et
successoribus suis . nomine perpetue vicarie percipiendas auctoritate dicti domini Willelmi et patris nostri assignantes
ad sustinendum omnia honera Episcopalia et sinodalia dictam ecclesiam contingentia . Saluis Dominis priori
et conventui sancti Andree omnibus aliis minutis decimis et rebus ad dictam ecclesiam peruenientibus cum
decima bladi . Et in huius rei testimonium presentes Litteras sigillorum nostrorum appositionibus duximus
roborandas. Valete.

p.cl: To all Christ’s faithful people who shall view or hear the present letters, Ranulf the dean of Lothian, John the parson of Pencaitland, Robert of Methven, and Richard of Kelso the rector of the church of Kinghorn, wish eternal salvation in the Lord.

Since by authority of our venerable father William by God’s grace bishop of St Andrews the valuation of the vicarage of Haddington was committed to us, according to the assessment of trustworthy men, who, having taken an oath, testified as far as this is concerned; having viewed and inspected the sworn testimonies, we have ordered the assessment be valued in the below-written portions, David of Berwick, then vicar, being present and consenting; that is in oblations and obventions of the whole parish in respect of the living and the dead as well from chapels as from the mother church, and all teinds of the business of burgesses and of all other merchants and conveyors, and all teinds of curtilages and orchards within the burgh with half of the teind of hay; assigning the same portions to him and his successors, by way of a perpetual vicarage, by the authority of our said lord and father, William, to support all episcopal burdens and synodal dues attached to the said church; all other lesser teinds and things coming to the said church, together with the teind of corn, being reserved to the lords prior and convent of St Andrews.
And in testimony of this matter we have caused the present letters to be verified by the application of our seals.
